key_evidence: Liberties.eu 2026 Rule of Law Report classifies Italy as one of five Dismantler states — 'systematically and intentionally' eroding rule of law across justice, anti-corruption, media freedom, balance of powers
Italy missed May 7 2026 deadline for transposition of EU anti-SLAPP Directive — EFJ, MFRR, ECPMF all issued statements May 8 calling on Italy to act urgently
CIVICUS Monitor downgraded Italy's civic space from 'narrowed' to 'obstructed' in 2025; SLAPPs against critics (including Meloni v. Saviano) documented

POSITIVE OFFSET: Italian voters rejected the Meloni government's Nordio judicial reform in constitutional referendum March 22-23 2026 (No 53.5%, Yes 46.5%, turnout 58%). Meloni admitted defeat — public democratic check against executive court restructuring
First-baseline Dismantler assessment with partial-offset convention applied — net delta -4.0 vs Slovakia -5.5 and Croatia -7.8, crediting the referendum positive
methodology_novel: POSITIVE-DEMOCRATIC-CHECK-AGAINST-ASSESSED-DISMANTLER — partial-offset convention established. When an entity is classified as a Dismantler by Tier-1 source (Liberties.eu) AND voters demonstrably block the executive's primary dismantling instrument via referendum within the assessment window, apply a -2.0 partial offset to the base Dismantler magnitude. Calibration: Slovakia -5.5 and Croatia -7.8 had no such offset (Slovakia's Fico media law was passed; Croatia's Plenković judicial appointments stood). Italy's referendum-blocked Nordio judicial reform is the inverse — democratic resilience demonstrated against the very dismantling instrument the Dismantler classification targets. Net Italy delta -4.0 from 50.0 (base -6.0 ≈ midway between Slovakia and Croatia for mid-Functional baseline, partial-offset +2.0 for referendum). Italy lands at 46.0, mid-Functional, 6.0pt above Developing/Functional boundary.
